markdown:
**TL;DR** — Claude Opus 5 shipped July 24 as a new state-of-the-art agentic model at unchanged Opus pricing, arriving the same week MCP 2026-07-28 finalizes as the protocol's largest revision ever.

## Anthropic and Claude
- **Claude Opus 5 launched July 24** — more than doubles Opus 4.8 on Frontier-Bench, triples the field on ARC-AGI 3, and beats Fable 5 on OSWorld 2.0 at one-third the cost; priced identically at $5/$25 per million tokens with a 2.5× fast-mode option. [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-5)
- **Claude Code 2.1.218 (July 22)**: `/code-review` now runs as a background subagent rather than blocking the session; screen reader and Windows path improvements also ship. [Changelog](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **Claude Code 2.1.212 (July 17)**: `/fork` copies the current conversation to a background session while you keep working; `/subtask` replaces in-session subagents with cleaner isolation — the most ergonomic workflow jump in the July run of releases. [Changelog](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **Auto mode on Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry** ships as opt-out default as of 2.1.207 (July 15), with subagent spawn limits (200/session) and WebSearch caps (200/session) added to prevent runaway agent costs. [Changelog](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **Anthropic Economic Index connector** (July 22) lets users query Claude directly against the economic index data — paired with a new Economic Futures Research Fund agenda published the same day. [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-economic-index-connector)

## NixOS ecosystem
- **Documentation team fundraising** (July 10): seeking €34,000 + €1,000/month for a paid maintainer; covers framework evaluation, page-splitting in nixos-render-docs, continuous deployment, and navigation/redirect work — meaningful investment in the historically rough onboarding story. [NixOS Blog](https://nixos.org/blog/announcements/2026/docs-funding-2026)
- **NixOS 26.05 "Yarara"** is current stable (released May 30); bugfix/security support runs through December 31, 2026. [NixOS Blog](https://nixos.org/blog/announcements/2026/nixos-2605)
- **26.11 milestone** is ~20% complete, targeting November 30; tracked work includes PCRE→PCRE2 migration, pnpm 9 EOL handling, and improved secret management — nothing dramatic yet, but the PCRE2 migration touches a large slice of the package graph. *(External: GitHub milestone, not an approved source)*

## AI coding agents and agent frameworks
- **MCP 2026-07-28 spec finalizes July 28** — the largest revision in the protocol's history: drops sessions and the initialization handshake, rewrites auth around OAuth/OIDC, introduces a Tasks extension for long-running work, and moves to a stateless HTTP core that runs on ordinary infrastructure; SDK maintainers have until the 28th to validate. *(External: [MCP Blog](https://blog.modelcontextprotocol.io/posts/2026-07-28-release-candidate/), [WorkOS](https://workos.com/blog/mcp-2026-spec-agent-authentication))*
- **Apple is suing OpenAI** over alleged IP/talent theft from former employees; Gruber likens it to Jobs-era recruitment aggression — material legal risk hanging over one of the dominant coding-agent providers. [Daring Fireball](https://daringfireball.net/2026/07/mornings_in_cupertino_have_the_aroma_of_napalm_once_again)
- **Linus Torvalds: "Linux is not one of those anti-AI projects"** — said he'd rather fork the project than restrict AI tool usage; relevant signal for open-source agent/tooling integration norms. *(Linked via Daring Fireball; primary source not directly fetched)*
- **Laguna XS 2.1** (Poolside, 33B MoE, agentic coding) and **Kimi K2.7 Code** (Moonshot AI) are the newest models added to the Ollama library this cycle. *(External: search results)*

## Self-hosting and homelab
- **Immich v3.0.3** released ~July 17 — bug-fix milestone for the self-hosted photo platform; Proxmox community scripts pinned it the same day. *(External: search results, not an approved source)*
- **MCP 2026-07-28 auth rewrite has direct homelab impact**: self-hosted MCP servers need to update their auth handling (OAuth/OIDC alignment) before the 12-month deprecation window closes; worth flagging now if you're running any local MCP infra. *(External)*
- No critical CVEs for major self-hosted stacks (Proxmox, Authentik, Vaultwarden, Docker) surfaced from available sources this week. Note: Ars Technica was unreachable this run — see source health block.

## Local and on-device LLM inference
- **Ollama v0.32.3 (July 23)**: fixes stalled model downloads; adds CUDA on Windows ARM64 and B200 support via CUDA 12. *(External: [Releasebot](https://releasebot.io/updates/ollama))*
- **Ollama v0.32.0 (July 14)**: running `ollama` bare now drops into an interactive local agent with chat, code, and web search; older coding models (CodeLlama, Llama 3.x, base DeepSeek-R1, Mistral, StarCoder) now show deprecation prompts before launching. *(External)*
- **Ollama MLX engine** (updated June 2026, rolling out through July): now the default inference path on Apple Silicon — produces higher-quality outputs, responds faster, and uses less memory than the previous llama.cpp backend on M-series hardware. *(External: search results)*
- **Llama 4 Scout** continues climbing Ollama's download charts since its April release; Laguna XS 2.1 is the new entrant to watch for agentic coding workloads. *(External: search results)*
